body:
In the second dispute, UMA voters ruled 99.5% "Early Request" — meaning the "No" proposal was submitted before the ceasefire deadline had actually passed, making it impossible to resolve the market at that point. This is critical: the "No" proposer lost their bond, and the outcome was NOT confirmed. The market did not resolve to No.
What "Early Request" means in practice:
— The previous "No" resolution is void
— The market resets and moves to Final Review
— UMA will now make a fresh, binding decision based on what actually happened
And what actually happened is clear: on April 22, hours before the ceasefire was set to expire, Trump announced an extension — a documented, widely reported fact across every major news outlet.
The Yes position was never properly evaluated. Final Review is the first real opportunity for UMA to rule on the facts. The facts support Yes.

1. Official Announcement and Confirmation According to reporting from major outlets (The Guardian, Al Jazeera, and the Council on Foreign Relations), President Donald Trump officially announced an extension of the two-week ceasefire on April 21, 2026. This announcement was made publicly via official channels (including Truth Social) and specifically directed the military to "extend the Ceasefire" while continuing the naval blockade.
2. Overwhelming Media ConsensusThe market rules state that resolution can be based on an "overwhelming consensus of media reporting" even if direct confirmation from both governments is complex.The New York Times and The Guardian reported that an extension was reached via Pakistani mediation.CFR and the House of Commons Library both summarized the status of the conflict as a "ceasefire (since extended)."While some Iranian officials (such as advisors to Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf) expressed skepticism or called the extension a "ploy," the global media reporting consistently categorized the situation as a formal extension of the April 7 agreement rather than a unilateral pause or informal understanding.

📞Telephone conversation between the Foreign Ministers of the Islamic Republic of Iran and Saudi Arabia

🔹Seyyed Abbas Araghchi and Faisal bin Farhan, Foreign Ministers of the Islamic Republic of Iran and Saudi Arabia, discussed and exchanged views on the latest regional developments and current diplomatic trends in a phone call.

🔹In this conversation, the Foreign Minister of our country explained various aspects of the current situation in the region, especially developments related to the ceasefire, and informed his Saudi counterpart about the latest efforts and diplomatic movements of the Islamic Republic of Iran in order to end the war and reduce tensions.
If you need a public statement from Iran about a ceasefire, then how about the fact that there wasn't a single fucking Iranian strike during that time?

Trump's April 21 Truth Social post IS a qualifying extension under the market rules. The rules state: "an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire extension agreement has been reached will suffice."
CNN, Al Jazeera, CNBC, Reuters, AP — every major outlet reported the ceasefire was extended. That IS the overwhelming consensus. The rules do NOT require Iran's signature — they explicitly offer the media consensus as an alternative to official statements.
Pakistan's PM Sharif publicly welcomed the extension and confirmed ongoing negotiations. Iran did not formally reject or denounce the extension — they continued operating under it.
Resolving this "No" ignores the plain text of the resolution criteria. This should be YES.

They 100% conflating requirements for clear communication by both governments with the separate test for media consensus, furthermore the disqualifying clauses of unilaterality are in reference to the ceasefire not the declaration of a ceasefire, if a ceasefire is only on one side by definition it is not a ceasefire, all cease fires are multilateral.

body: what is UMA?

If/when this disputes, UMA voters examining the record will find:

Trump unilateral announcement (clearly unilateral per his own wording)
Pakistan PM thank-you tweet (third-party mediator, not Iran government)
NYT framing as Pakistan "courting" and "seeking to mediate" (not validating)
Iran officials publicly rejecting (Ghalibaf adviser, Araghchi, negotiating team)
Press TV / Iran International / Tehran Times reporting rejection
Blockade continuing (a stated Iran claim of ceasefire violation)
No joint statement exists
Iran refusing Islamabad talks as of 21:07 GMT+1
This is a strong NO case on rule-text.

Agreed, strongly agreed. Rules are clear as a day; it has to be the same playbook as two weeks ago to qualify (which it clearly isn't). However, there is big money to be lost on the yes side, and these ppl won't give up easily, likely disputing the outcome if NO or proposing yes otherwise.

body: No, that is false. Trump declared the ceasing unilaterally, pick whatever news outlet you like. And then Iran outright rejected the ceasefire, said by their own spokesman. Another matter is that they choose not to attack, but they agreed to nothing.

🇮🇷🇺🇸🚨 Iran agrees to extend the ceasefire

💢 Iran has accepted Trump’s request, conveyed through Pakistan, to extend the ceasefire.

💢 Esmail Baghaei, spokesperson for Iran’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs, confirmed the extension of the ceasefire. However, he did not mention anything about participating in a new round of negotiations with the United States in Islamabad.

"No final decision has been made on whether Iran will take part in peace talks... contradictory messages, inconsistent behavior, and unacceptable actions of the American side"


Iran's actions by the United States constitute "maritime piracy and state terrorism"

"No meeting is planned to take place between Iran and the US"
